title: salix nigricans subsp. alpicola
intro: Salix nigricans subsp. alpicola, also known as the alpine blackwillow, is a subspecies of Salix nigricans, a type of willow tree native to the alpine regions of Europe. The bark and leaves of Salix nigricans subsp. alpicola contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it a valuable medicinal plant. The health benefits of Salix nigricans subsp. alpicola include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as an antiseptic and astringent. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of rheumatism, fever, and skin conditions.
